D2 Vision L3 Apply Reference

The Bezold Spreading Effect

slide the base colour and watch black lines darken it while white lines lighten the very same hue

At fine scale the visual system blurs neighbours together rather than contrasting them. Thin black or white lines bleed into a colour, dragging it darker or lighter.

Both halves are painted the identical base colour. Slide the hue and compare: the half laced with black lines sinks darker, the half laced with white lines floats lighter — assimilation, the opposite of contrast.
